title: Antimicrobial compositions and use thereof
abstract: Compositions comprising crosslinked polymers comprising one or more volatile oils and uses same for e.g., food preservation is disclosed.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A composition-of-matter comprising a polymer having incorporated within an antimicrobial effective amount of one or more volatile oils, wherein said polymer has surface area of 0.1 to 4 m 2 /g. 2. The composition of claim 1 , wherein said polymer has surface area of 0.4 to 2 m 2 /g. 3. The composition of claim 1, wherein said polymer has a pore volume of 0.001 to 0.01 cm 3 /g. 4. The composition of claim 1 , wherein said antimicrobial effective amount of one or more volatile oils is at least 1% by total weight of said composition. 5. The composition-of-matter of claim 1, wherein a weight ratio of said oil and said polymer is in a range of between 5 to 60. 6. The composition of claim 1, wherein at least 1% of said oil is non-covalently incorporated within said polymer. 7. The composition of claim 1, wherein at least 1% of said oil does not undergo oxidization within said polymer for a pre-determined time. 8. The composition-of-matter of claim 1, wherein said one or more volatile oils are characterized by vapor pressure of at least about 0.001 mmHg at about 4-7 Â°C. 9. The composition-of-matter of claim 1, wherein said one or more volatile oils are selected from the group consisting of oregano, carvacrol and thymol, lemon grass, or isomers, derivatives and mixtures thereof. 10. The composition-of-matter of claim 1, wherein said one or more volatile oils have an antimicrobial effect. 11. The composition of claim 1 , wherein said polymer is an amphiphilic polymer. 12. The composition of claim 1, wherein said polymer is a hydrophobic polymer. 13. The composition of claim 1 wherein said polymer is a cross-linked polymer. 14. The composition-of-matter of claim 1, wherein said polymer is selected from the group consisting of polyacrylic acid, carbohydrate, polyallyl methacrylates, cellulose, starch, maltodextrin and mixtures, copolymers or derivatives thereof. 15. The composition-of-matter of any claim 14, wherein said cellulose is selected from the group consisting of: microcrystalline cellulose and croscarmellose. 16. The composition-of-matter of any one the proceeding claims, being in a form of a patch or a tablet. 17. The composition-of-matter of claim 16, wherein the patch or the tablet are in the form of a single layer. 18. The composition-of-matter of claim 16, wherein the patch or the tablet are in the form of a double layer. 19. The composition-of-matter of any one of claims 17 and 18, comprising at least one layer comprising one or more adhesive materials. 20. The composition-of-matter of any one of claim 16, wherein said polymer and said oil are compressed thereby forming the patch or tablet. 21. The composition-of-matter of any one of claims 1 to 20 further comprising any one of a flavoring agent, a binder, a lubricant, or a combination thereof. 22. The composition-of-matter of any one of claims 1 to 21, for use as a preservative activity. 23. An article comprising the composition-of-matter of any one of claims 1-22. 24. The article of claim 22, being selected from the group consisting of a food package, and medical device package. 25. A method of inhibiting or reducing a formation of load of a microorganism and/or a formation of a biofilm, within an article, the method comprising incorporating the composition-of-matter of any one of claims 1-20 within said article. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein said microorganism is selected from bacteria, molds and fungi. 27. A method for the preparation of an antimicrobial composition, the method comprising: (i) providing a polymer having a surface area of 0.1 to 4 m 2 /g; 

 (ii) providing an antimicrobial effective amount of one or more volatile oils; and (iii) saturating said polymer with the antimicrobial effective amount of one or more volatile oils. 28. The method of claim 27, further comprising a step of pressing the saturated polymer under a pressure of at least 1 ton.
